method: Per-doc HT register scores (justif, h_size, h_len); coherence C = mean|pairwise Spearman|; independence null = permute each column independently (1000 draws, tie-aware); size-controlled via OLS residualization on log(n_glyphs); synthetic positive control (coherent-template DETECT arm + independent FALSE-POSITIVE arm, 15 reps each).
key_findings: The three HT layout registers (justif, h_size, h_len) are NOT statistically independent: observed coherence C_obs=0.160 exceeds the per-column-independence null (perm_p=0.007, 1000 draws, tie-aware).

The dependence is MIXED-SIGN, not a coherent all-positive formal template: signed pairwise Spearman r are -0.143 (justif~h_size), +0.149 (justif~h_len), -0.189 (h_size~h_len). Two of three pairs are negative.

The dominant structure is a heading-size <-> header-length TRADEOFF (r=-0.189): documents whose first row has larger glyphs have SHORTER first rows (fewer glyphs). This is the opposite of an all-positive template in which 'formal' docs would show large AND long headers.
Size control (residualizing each score on log(n_glyphs)) leaves the dependence intact: C_sc=0.156, perm_p_sc=0.011. The co-occurrence is not an artifact of document size.

Positive control PASSED: the machinery DETECTS a synthetic coherent template (power=1.0 over 15 reps, median detect_p=0.001) and does NOT fire on synthetic independent columns (false-positive rate=0.067 <= 0.10). The dependence finding is calibrated, not a machinery artifact.
successor_hypotheses: E088: Characterize the h_size<->h_len tradeoff curve within HT — is it a continuous negative slope or a bimodal split (a class of 'big-glyph short-header' docs vs 'small-glyph long-header' docs)? Cluster the per-doc (h_size,h_len) plane.

E089: Test whether the h_size<->h_len tradeoff reflects a fixed ink/area budget per header row (large glyphs cost area, so fewer fit) — predict h_len from total first-row ink area and compare to the empirical slope.
E090: Replicate the three-register coherence test at OTHER Linear A sites (e.g., Knossos, Phaistos) to see whether the same mixed-sign dependence is HT-specific or a pan-Line-A scribal regularity.
E091: Add a FOURTH register (e.g., inter-row spacing regularity or column-block alignment) and re-test coherence; does adding registers raise C (more dependence) or dilute it?

E092: Within HT, test whether the tradeoff class correlates with support type (tablet vs other) or document length tier — i.e., is the big-glyph-short-header pattern a genre marker rather than a layout template?
E093: Permute the THREE columns JOINTLY (same permutation) vs independently to decompose how much of C comes from a single shared axis vs pairwise-specific structure.
non_circular: All scores are pure geometry of bbox glyphs (left/right edges, glyph heights/areas, row counts) under opaque doc IDs; no phonetic or semantic sign values are read. The independence null permutes each register column separately, preserving each register's marginal distribution while destroying cross-register co-occurrence, so significance cannot arise from a single register's internal structure. The positive control is fully synthetic (no Linear A data), gating the verdict on calibrated machinery. Prior epochs E081/E085/E086 each measured ONE register independently; this epoch tests their joint co-occurrence, so it does not re-use their per-register conclusions as inputs.
deviations: Data path resolved to experiments/linear_a_frontier_72h/data/sigla_glyphs_bbox.json (the PATH CONTRACT referenced BASE/data/... which does not exist in the working tree); same file, same schema.
Positive-control n_target set to max(n_docs, 128) = 128 to match the real-doc sample size; matches the preregistered ~128.
